com.mentor.chs.api.icd

Interface IXICDProvider

  • All Known Subinterfaces:
    IXApplicationContext

    @NotToBeImplemented
public interface IXICDProvider

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.util.Set<? extends IXICD> getApplicableICDs(IXDesign design)
      Gets a collection of ICDs 'applicable' to the specified design.
      java.util.Set<? extends IXICD> getICDsByNameAndRevision(java.lang.String name, java.lang.String revision)
      Gets a collection of ICDs for which the ICD name equals the specified name and the ICD revision equals the specified revision.
      ICD is uniquely determined by its 'name' and 'revision'.
      java.util.Set<? extends IXICD> getICDsByRole(java.lang.String role)
      Gets the collection of ICDs whose 'role' equals the specified role.

    • Method Detail

      • getApplicableICDs

        java.util.Set<? extends IXICD> getApplicableICDs(IXDesign design)
        Gets a collection of ICDs 'applicable' to the specified design. See XICDTagType for applicability.
        Parameters:
        design - A design
        Returns:
        Set<IXICD>, Set of ICDs

      • getICDsByNameAndRevision

        java.util.Set<? extends IXICD> getICDsByNameAndRevision(java.lang.String name,
                                                        java.lang.String revision)
        Gets a collection of ICDs for which the ICD name equals the specified name and the ICD revision equals the specified revision.
        ICD is uniquely determined by its 'name' and 'revision'. 'name' and 'revision' are mandatory attributes of an ICD. If the specified 'revision' is null/empty, the system will get all the ICDs with name as the specified name. If the specified name is null/empty, an empty list is returned.
        Parameters:
        name - Name of an ICD
        revision - Revision of an ICD. This can be empty or null.
        Returns:
        Set<IXICD>, Set of ICDs

      • getICDsByRole

        java.util.Set<? extends IXICD> getICDsByRole(java.lang.String role)
        Gets the collection of ICDs whose 'role' equals the specified role.
        Parameters:
        role - role
        Returns:
        Set<IXICD>, Set of ICDs
